edges.description: Reduced cAMP responsiveness weakens PTH, TSH, and related hormonal signaling.
Blunted PKA signaling disrupts chondrocyte maturation and endochondral ossification.
Impaired signal termination blunts adaptation to PTH/TSH stimulation.
Aberrant cAMP kinetics disrupt skeletal growth signaling.
Hormone resistance contributes to impaired growth and short stature.
Endocrine signaling defects compound skeletal developmental abnormalities.
Growth plate dysfunction shortens metacarpals and phalanges.
Abnormal endochondral ossification produces cone-shaped epiphyses.
Impaired longitudinal growth lowers adult height.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the phenotypic spectrum of variants in PDE4D/PRKAR1A: from acrodysostosis to acroscyphodysplasia.
evidence.snippet: Acrodysostosis (MIM 101800) is a dominantly inherited condition associating (1) skeletal features (short stature, facial dysostosis, and brachydactyly with cone-shaped epiphyses), (2) resistance to hormones and (3) possible intellectual disability.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the phenotypic spectrum of variants in PDE4D/PRKAR1A: from acrodysostosis to acroscyphodysplasia.

nodes.description: Disrupted cAMP/PKA signaling impairs growth plate chondrocyte architecture and endochondral ossification, producing the characteristic skeletal phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: Correction of a knock-in mouse model of acrodysostosis with gene therapy using a rAAV9-CAG-human PRKAR1A vector.
evidence.snippet: Chondrocyte architecture was restored in the growth plates.

evidence.reference_title: Correction of a knock-in mouse model of acrodysostosis with gene therapy using a rAAV9-CAG-human PRKAR1A vector.
evidence.snippet: Vector-mediated hPRKAR1A expression was found in growth plate chondrocytes

nodes.description: Resistance to parathyroid hormone and thyroid-stimulating hormone is a common biochemical consequence of disrupted Gsα/cAMP/PKA signaling in acrodysostosis type 1 (PRKAR1A). Hormone resistance is absent or minimal in acrodysostosis type 2 (PDE4D).

evidence.reference_title: Acrodysostosis and pseudohypoparathyroidism (PHP): adaptation of Japanese patients with a newly proposed classification and expanding the phenotypic spectrum of variants.
evidence.snippet: Group B consisted of six patients who showed hormone resistance without hypocalcemia.

evidence.reference_title: Multiple hormone resistance and alterations of G-protein-coupled receptors signaling.
evidence.snippet: resistance to hormones such as PTH, TSH, gonadotropins and GHRH may be variably present, so that the clinical and molecular overlap among these different but related disorders represents a challenge for endocrinologists as to differential diagnosis and genetic counseling.

nodes.description: PDE4D missense variants alter PDE4D-mediated cAMP hydrolysis and signal termination, weakening the reset of PKA activation cycles.

evidence.reference_title: A novel variant in the PDE4D gene is the cause of Acrodysostosis type 2 in a Lithuanian patient: a case report.
evidence.snippet: This variant causes amino acid change (NP_001098101.1:p.(Arg194Pro)) in the functionally relevant upstream conserved region 1 domain of PDE4D.

evidence.reference_title: A novel de novo PDE4D gene mutation identified in a Chinese patient with acrodysostosis.
evidence.snippet: Further functional analysis showed that the p.Tyr677Ser substitution changes the function of the PDE4D protein, affects its subcellular localization in transfected cells, increases PDE4 activity in the regulation of cAMP signaling and affects cell proliferation.

evidence.reference_title: Impaired cAMP processivity by phosphodiesterase-protein kinase A complexes in acrodysostosis.
evidence.snippet: Mutations in the 3'5'cyclic AMP (cAMP)-dependent protein kinase (PKA) type I regulatory subunit isoform α (RIα) and phosphodiesterase (PDE) PDE4D have both been implicated in impaired PKA regulation in acrodysostosis.

nodes.description: PRKAR1A truncating and C-terminal variants reduce cAMP-dependent PKA activation and create cAMP-resistant holoenzymes, impairing downstream signal transduction.

evidence.reference_title: Structure of a PKA RIα Recurrent Acrodysostosis Mutant Explains Defective cAMP-Dependent Activation.
evidence.snippet: ACRDYS1 mutations result in decreased PKA activity and cAMP resistant holoenzymes.

evidence.reference_title: Recurrent Acrodysostosis-Related PKA RIα Mutant Reveals a Novel Mechanism of Aberrant PKA Deactivation.
evidence.snippet: Several mutations in the ubiquitous RIα isoform of R cause Acrodysostosis 1 (ACRO), a disease characterized by resistance to thyroid-stimulating and parathyroid hormones leading to severe congenital malformations.

evidence.reference_title: Growth patterns and outcomes of growth hormone therapy in patients with acrodysostosis.
evidence.snippet: Severe short stature is a feature of acrodysostosis, but data on growth are sparse.
